textPlain: More power to him and I hope he makes it but ambitious action against climate change this is not. Under no circumstances will modified paddleboards become standard modes of transportation nor will anyone with the ability to make change look at this as something other than a rich dude risking their life.
I don't see the point beyond feeding one's ego. I think he does it because such experiences brings in money in his talks.
Seems like a really inefficient way to paddle?
It's rather stretching the definition of a SUP if you ask me, but fair winds and following seas to him anyway.
wouldn't the  current negate the paddling as you go farther out?

Depends on the crossing. Usually they will choose a route that is favorable. This is probably the same as the ocean crossings by row boat.https://youtu.be/6SYHamnHqU8?si=vp-NLeMnsdQz1R-ZGenerally speaking there is a route called the "Milk Run" from Europe to the Caribbean that has tail winds and good currents in the right direction. Also much nicer weather.
